title: दत्तात्रेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली
devanagariText:
॥ श्री दत्तात्रेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली ॥

ॐ दत्ताय नमः।
ॐ दत्तात्रेयाय नमः।
ॐ अवधूताय नमः।
ॐ अत्रिपुत्राय नमः।
ॐ अनसूयासुताय नमः।
ॐ महायोगिने नमः।
ॐ महात्मने नमः।
ॐ अवधूतेन्द्राय नमः।
ॐ जगद्गुरवे नमः।
ॐ देवदेवाय नमः॥ १०॥

ॐ त्रिमूर्तये नमः।
ॐ ब्रह्मविष्णुमहेश्वराय नमः।
ॐ सर्वज्ञाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वशक्तये नमः।
ॐ सर्वव्यापिने नमः।
ॐ सर्वगाय नमः।
ॐ दिगम्बराय नमः।
ॐ शान्तमूर्तये नमः।
ॐ ज्ञानमूर्तये नमः।
ॐ योगमूर्तये नमः॥ २०॥

ॐ निर्गुणाय नमः।
ॐ सगुणाय नमः।
ॐ साक्षिणे नमः।
ॐ परमात्मने नमः।
ॐ सच्चिदानन्दाय नमः।
ॐ ब्रह्मस्वरूपाय नमः।
ॐ कृपानिधये नमः।
ॐ भक्तवत्सलाय नमः।
ॐ शरणागतवत्सलाय नमः।
ॐ दीनबन्धवे नमः॥ ३०॥

ॐ चतुर्बाहवे नमः।
ॐ त्रिमुखाय नमः।
ॐ षडानुजाय नमः।
ॐ कमण्डलुधराय नमः।
ॐ शूलधराय नमः।
ॐ चक्रधराय नमः।
ॐ शङ्खधराय नमः।
ॐ डमरुधराय नमः।
ॐ गदाधराय नमः।
ॐ पद्मधराय नमः॥ ४०॥

ॐ कालज्ञाय नमः।
ॐ कलिमलहराय नमः।
ॐ सिद्धेश्वराय नमः।
ॐ योगेश्वराय नमः।
ॐ ज्ञानेश्वराय नमः।
ॐ धर्मेश्वराय नमः।
ॐ अनाथनाथाय नमः।
ॐ परमेश्वराय नमः।
ॐ स्मृतिगामिने नमः।
ॐ स्मृतिमात्रसन्तुष्टाय नमः॥ ५०॥

ॐ नवनाथाय नमः।
ॐ चौरासीसिद्धगुरवे नमः।
ॐ श्वानवृत्ताय नमः।
ॐ धेनुसेव्याय नमः।
ॐ वृक्षवासिने नमः।
ॐ गिरिवासिने नमः।
ॐ तीर्थवासिने नमः।
ॐ सर्ववासिने नमः।
ॐ चतुर्विंशतिगुरवे नमः।
ॐ अवधूतगीताप्रवक्त्रे नमः॥ ६०॥

ॐ जीवन्मुक्ताय नमः।
ॐ विदेहमुक्ताय नमः।
ॐ सन्यासिने नमः।
ॐ परमहंसाय नमः।
ॐ तपस्विने नमः।
ॐ ब्रह्मचारिणे नमः।
ॐ गृहस्थाय नमः।
ॐ वानप्रस्थाय नमः।
ॐ आश्रमत्रयपूज्याय नमः।
ॐ वर्णत्रयपूज्याय नमः॥ ७०॥

ॐ योगिराजाय नमः।
ॐ विरक्ताय नमः।
ॐ वैराग्यनिधये नमः।
ॐ ज्ञानसागराय नमः।
ॐ भक्तिप्रदाय नमः।
ॐ मुक्तिप्रदाय नमः।
ॐ सिद्धिप्रदाय नमः।
ॐ बुद्धिप्रदाय नमः।
ॐ ऐश्वर्यप्रदाय नमः।
ॐ शान्तिप्रदाय नमः॥ ८०॥

ॐ गुरुगुरवे नमः।
ॐ आदिगुरवे नमः।
ॐ सद्गुरवे नमः।
ॐ परमगुरवे नमः।
ॐ ज्ञानगम्याय नमः।
ॐ भक्तिगम्याय नमः।
ॐ ध्यानगम्याय नमः।
ॐ सेवागम्याय नमः।
ॐ उपदेशगम्याय नमः।
ॐ कृपागम्याय नमः॥ ९०॥

ॐ अनन्ताय नमः।
ॐ अव्ययाय नमः।
ॐ अक्षराय नमः।
ॐ अमराय नमः।
ॐ अजराय नमः।
ॐ शाश्वताय नमः।
ॐ नित्याय नमः।
ॐ सुखरूपाय नमः।
ॐ आनन्दरूपाय नमः।
ॐ चैतन्यरूपाय नमः॥ १००॥

ॐ भस्मोद्धूलितविग्रहाय नमः।
ॐ रुद्राक्षमालाधराय नमः।
ॐ कृष्णमृगचर्मधराय नमः।
ॐ गङ्गाधराय नमः।
ॐ अमृतवर्षिणे नमः।
ॐ ब्रह्मानन्दाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वसिद्धिप्रदाय नमः।
ॐ श्रीदत्तात्रेयपरब्रह्मणे नमः॥ १०८॥

॥ इति श्री दत्तात्रेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Dattatreya Ashtottara Shatanamavali ||

Om Dattaya Namah.
Om Dattatreyaya Namah.
Om Avadhutaya Namah.
Om Atriputraya Namah.
Om Anasuyasutaya Namah.
Om Mahayogine Namah.
Om Mahatmane Namah.
Om Avadhutendra Namah.
Om Jagadgurave Namah.
Om Devadevaya Namah. || 10 ||

Om Trimurtaye Namah.
Om Brahma-Vishnu-Maheshwaraya Namah.
Om Sarvajnaya Namah.
Om Sarvashaktaye Namah.
Om Sarvavyapine Namah.
Om Sarvagaya Namah.
Om Digambaraya Namah.
Om Shantamurtaye Namah.
Om Jnanamurtaye Namah.
Om Yogamurtaye Namah. || 20 ||

Om Nirgunaya Namah.
Om Sagunaya Namah.
Om Sakshine Namah.
Om Paramatmane Namah.
Om Sachchidanandaya Namah.
Om Brahma-svarupaya Namah.
Om Kripanidhaye Namah.
Om Bhaktavatsalaya Namah.
Om Sharanagata-vatsalaya Namah.
Om Dinabandhave Namah. || 30 ||

Om Chaturbahave Namah.
Om Trimukhaya Namah.
Om Shadanujaya Namah.
Om Kamandalu-dharaya Namah.
Om Shuladharaya Namah.
Om Chakradharaya Namah.
Om Shankhadharaya Namah.
Om Damarudharaya Namah.
Om Gadadharaya Namah.
Om Padmadharaya Namah. || 40 ||

Om Kalajnaya Namah.
Om Kalimala-haraya Namah.
Om Siddheshwaraya Namah.
Om Yogeshwaraya Namah.
Om Jnaneshwaraya Namah.
Om Dharmeshwaraya Namah.
Om Anathanathaya Namah.
Om Parameshwaraya Namah.
Om Smritigamine Namah.
Om Smritimatra-santushtaya Namah. || 50 ||

Om Navanathaya Namah.
Om Chaurasi-siddha-gurave Namah.
Om Shvana-vrittaya Namah.
Om Dhenu-sevyaya Namah.
Om Vriksha-vasine Namah.
Om Giri-vasine Namah.
Om Tirtha-vasine Namah.
Om Sarva-vasine Namah.
Om Chaturvimshati-gurave Namah.
Om Avadhuta-gita-pravaktre Namah. || 60 ||

Om Jivanmuktaya Namah.
Om Videhamuktaya Namah.
Om Sanyasine Namah.
Om Parahamsaya Namah.
Om Tapasvine Namah.
Om Brahmacharine Namah.
Om Grihasthaya Namah.
Om Vanaprasthaya Namah.
Om Ashramatraya-pujyaya Namah.
Om Varnatraya-pujyaya Namah. || 70 ||

Om Yogirajaya Namah.
Om Viraktaya Namah.
Om Vairagyanidhaye Namah.
Om Jnanasagaraya Namah.
Om Bhaktipradaya Namah.
Om Muktipradaya Namah.
Om Siddhipradaya Namah.
Om Buddhipradaya Namah.
Om Aishvaryapradaya Namah.
Om Shantipradaya Namah. || 80 ||

Om Gurugurave Namah.
Om Adigurave Namah.
Om Sadgurave Namah.
Om Paramagurave Namah.
Om Jnanagamyaya Namah.
Om Bhaktigamyaya Namah.
Om Dhyanagamyaya Namah.
Om Sevagamyaya Namah.
Om Upadeshagamyaya Namah.
Om Kripagamyaya Namah. || 90 ||

Om Anantaya Namah.
Om Avyayaya Namah.
Om Aksharaya Namah.
Om Amaraya Namah.
Om Ajaraya Namah.
Om Shashvataya Namah.
Om Nityaya Namah.
Om Sukharupaya Namah.
Om Anandarupaya Namah.
Om Chaitanyarupaya Namah. || 100 ||

Om Bhasmoddhulita-vigrahaya Namah.
Om Rudraksha-mala-dharaya Namah.
Om Krishna-mriga-charma-dharaya Namah.
Om Gangadharaya Namah.
Om Amritavarshine Namah.
Om Brahmanandaya Namah.
Om Sarvasiddhi-pradaya Namah.
Om Shri-Dattatreya-Parabrahmane Namah. || 108 ||

|| Iti Shri Dattatreya Ashtottara Shatanamavali Sampoorna ||
hindiMeaning: दत्तात्रेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली में भगवान दत्तात्रेय के 108 पवित्र नामों का संग्रह है। दत्तात्रेय ब्रह्मा, विष्णु, महेश्वर तीनों की त्रिमूर्ति हैं, अत्रि ऋषि और अनसूया माता के पुत्र हैं। वे अवधूत, जगद्गुरु, योगीश्वर, ज्ञानेश्वर, दिगम्बर, परमहंस और चौबीस गुरुओं से ज्ञान लेने वाले आदिगुरु हैं। पूजा-अर्चना में 108 नामों से पुष्पार्पण के लिए यह नामावली अत्यन्त उपयोगी है।
benefits: Perfect for archana worship offering 108 names to Dattatreya
Traditionally recited for removal of kalimala (impurities of kali yuga)
Invokes the blessing of bhakti, mukti, siddhi, buddhi, and aishvarya
Especially powerful on Dattatreya Jayanti and Thursdays
description: The Dattatreya Ashtottara Shatanamavali, known in Sanskrit as दत्तात्रेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली, is a devotional recitation of 108 sacred names in praise of Lord Dattatreya. Rooted in Dattatreya Tantra, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow bhakti, mukti, siddhi, buddhi, and aishvarya and grant guru-kripa and spiritual wisdom. It is also said to remove kalimala (impurities of Kali Yuga). It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Datta Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. With 108 verses and a reading time of about 12 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Dattatreya Ashtottara Shatanamavali in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Dattatreya Ashtottara Shatanamavali - 108 names of Lord Dattatreya for archana in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning.
relatedSearches: Dattatreya 108 names lyrics in Sanskrit Marathi PDF
Datta Ashtottara archana flowers Tulasi Bilva
108 names of Dattatreya meaning Hindi English
Datta archana procedure Thursday Datta peetha

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dattatreya Ashtottara Shatanamavali?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dattatreya Ashtottara Shatanamavali (दत्तात्रेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली) is a sacred recitation of 108 divine names of Lord Dattatreya — the unified incarnation of the Hindu Trinity (Brahma-Vishnu-Maheshvara) and the foremost Adi-Guru of the Avadhuta and Yoga lineages. Each name is chanted with the prefix Om and suffix Namah, traditionally recited during archana — the offering of flowers, tulsi, bilva leaves, or kumkum at the feet of Datta with each name. The 108 names trace Datta's cosmic identity as Trimurti, his birth from Anasuya and Atri, his Avadhuta nature (Digambara), his role as the universal Sadguru, his association with the four traditional symbols (kamandalu, shula, chakra, shankha), his disposition as Smriti-matra-santushta (satisfied by mere remembrance), and his bestowal of bhakti, mukti, siddhi, buddhi, and aishvarya. (Sources: Dattatreya Tantra; Datta Sampradaya archana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the most important groups of names in this Datta Namavali?
seoFaqs.answer: The 108 names of Datta cluster into clear theological groups: (1) Lineage names — Datta, Dattatreya, Atriputra, Anasuyasuta (born of Atri and Anasuya); (2) Trimurti names — Trimurtaye, Brahma-Vishnu-Maheshwaraya, Sarvajna, Sarvashakti; (3) Avadhuta-Yogi names — Avadhuta, Mahayogi, Yogiraja, Digambara, Parahamsa, Sanyasi; (4) Form-iconography names — Chaturbahave (four-armed), Trimukha (three-faced), Shadanuja (with six dogs), Kamandalu-dhara, Shula-dhara, Chakra-dhara, Shankha-dhara, Damaru-dhara; (5) Guru names — Jagadguru, Adiguru, Sadguru, Paramaguru, Chaturvimshati-guru (the 24 gurus), Avadhuta-Gita-pravakta; (6) Boon-bestowing names — Bhaktiprada, Muktiprada, Siddhiprada, Buddhiprada, Aishvaryaprada, Shantiprada; (7) Compassionate names — Smriti-gami, Smriti-matra-santushta, Bhaktavatsala, Sharanagata-vatsala, Dinabandhu. (Sources: Dattatreya Ashtottara text; Datta Sampradaya theology.)

seoFaqs.question: Who are the 24 Gurus (Chaturvimshati Guru) of Dattatreya?
seoFaqs.answer: Among Datta's most distinctive names is Chaturvimshati-guru — the one who took 24 gurus from nature. The story is narrated in Bhagavata Purana (Skandha 11, chapters 7-9) where Lord Krishna recounts to Uddhava the conversation between Avadhuta Dattatreya and King Yadu. Datta tells the king he learned wisdom from 24 teachers in the natural world: (1) Earth — patience and forbearance; (2) Air — non-attachment; (3) Sky — equanimity; (4) Water — purity; (5) Fire — purity through tapas; (6) Moon — change without affecting the self; (7) Sun — gathering for distribution; (8) Pigeon — danger of attachment; (9) Python — contentment with what comes; (10) Ocean — depth and unchanging nature; (11) Moth — danger of sense-attraction; (12) Bee — collecting essence; (13) Honey-gatherer — futility of hoarding; (14) Elephant — dangers of lust; (15) Deer — danger of music-attraction; (16) Fish — danger of taste; (17) Pingala the courtesan — disillusionment; (18) Kurara bird — non-possession; (19) Child — innocence; (20) Maiden — solitude; (21) Arrow-maker — concentration; (22) Snake — solitary dwelling; (23) Spider — projection-withdrawal of universe; (24) Wasp — becoming what one meditates on. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 11.7-11.9.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Smriti-matra-santushta in the Datta Namavali?
seoFaqs.answer: Smriti-matra-santushta (स्मृतिमात्रसन्तुष्ट) — meaning the one who is satisfied (santushta) by mere remembrance (smriti-matra) — is one of the most theologically distinctive names of Lord Dattatreya in the Ashtottara. Unlike most deities who are pleased only after elaborate puja, mantra-japa, abhisheka, or pilgrimage, Datta is satisfied simply by being remembered. This is paired with the related name Smriti-gami — one who comes (gami) to the rememberer (smartri). Together these two names express the unique Datta theology: the moment a sincere devotee remembers him, Datta arrives. There is no need for elaborate ritual, social qualification, or formal initiation. This makes Datta the most universally accessible deity in the Hindu pantheon — particularly cherished by householders, the poor, the marginalized, and those without access to formal religious infrastructure. (Sources: Dattatreya Ashtottara names 49-50; Datta Sampradaya theology.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Dattatreya Ashtottara archana traditionally performed?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical Datta archana procedure is: (1) wake before sunrise, take a ritual bath, wear clean clothes (preferably white or saffron); (2) sit before a Datta murti, picture, or paduka, facing east or north; (3) light a ghee deepam and incense; (4) perform sankalpa stating the date, place, and purpose; (5) offer ganesha-vandana, guru-vandana, and Datta dhyana; (6) chant the 108 names slowly and clearly, offering at each name either tulasi leaves, bilva leaves, audumbara (cluster fig) leaves, white or red flowers, or akshat-kumkum; (7) conclude with the Digambara dhun (Digambara Digambara Sripada Vallabha Digambara) chanted 108 times; (8) offer naivedya (preferably kanda-mool, sweet rice, or simple sattvic food); (9) feed Brahmins or the poor (Datta-prasad-dana); (10) perform pradakshina and namaskara. The archana is especially powerful on Thursdays, on every Margashirsha-shukla-ekadashi, and on Datta Jayanti. (Sources: Datta Sampradaya archana paddhati; Datta peetha tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits are attributed to recitation of the Datta Ashtottara Shatanamavali?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following benefits to sincere recitation of the Datta 108 names: removal of kalimala (impurities of the Kali Yuga) — Datta as Kalimala-hara is specifically invoked to cleanse the karmic impurities accumulated in this dark age; bestowal of guru-kripa and reception of a true Sadguru in life; awakening of bhakti (devotion), mukti (liberation), siddhi (spiritual powers), buddhi (wisdom), and aishvarya (prosperity) — the panchavidha grace that Datta uniquely bestows; protection from black magic, evil spirits, and graha-doshas; removal of all diseases through Datta's role as mahayogi and ayurveda-acharya; and the supreme phala — Datta-darshan and admission to the Datta Parampara. The Ashtottara is especially powerful when recited daily on Thursdays during the 108-day Datta vrat. (Sources: Datta Tantra phalashruti; Datta Sampradaya tradition.)
